note: Curated hall-of-fame (editor's picks). Seeded with the lab's own work to show the bar — your contribution can replace these. Winners are chosen by hand from submissions; we publish the approach that actually worked, not just the idea.

lanes.title: Best question
lanes.blurb: A sharp question we hadn't answered — plus the approach that cracked it.

entries.title: Which clean beaches will suddenly spike?
entries.what: Instead of 'is the water dirty now,' ask 'which currently-clean beaches are about to surprise us.'
entries.approach: Reframe the target to clean-to-dirty onset, pool stations into one model, then use physics-pi as the surviving booster.
entries.result: Clean-to-dirty onset beats station memory; physics-pi raises AP 0.2992 to 0.3348, while tensor is not promoted.

lanes.title: Best suggestion
lanes.blurb: Something we should try — a signal, an objective, a basin, a baseline.

entries.title: Try harmonic spring-neap tidal range as a driver.
entries.what: Most tide features wash; the spring-neap envelope might carry independent signal.
entries.approach: Harmonic decomposition, leave-one-basin-out, permutation null before believing it.
entries.result: Confirmed escape: +0.011 AP (perm-null p=0.02); shipped, moved the headline to 0.5099.

lanes.title: Best contribution
lanes.blurb: Data, code, a label set, or a reproduction we can fold straight in.

entries.title: Ported the nowcast abroad with Irish + EU bathing-water data.
entries.what: Fetched Ireland EPA per-sample FIB (24.3k) + EEA pan-EU annual class (225k rows, 22k sites).
entries.approach: Standard fetch → silver → zero-shot transfer test with permutation null and local recalibration.
entries.result: CA nowcast transfers zero-shot to Ireland as a ranker (AP 0.122, ~4.3×, CI-separated).
